file_name: HEMISPHERX - Sales, Marketing, Distribution, and Supply Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: "Product" means an injectable formulation of clinical grade Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived). {***} Confidential portions of this exhibit have been redacted and filed separately with the Commission pursuant to a confidential treatment request in accordance with Rule 24b-2 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. Page 1 of 28 "Product Data" means all data possessed by HEMISPHERX relating to the use of Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived) to treat patients in the Field and which is needed to obtain regulatory approval in the Territory.
perturbation.changed_text: "Product" means an injectable formulation of clinical grade Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived). For the avoidance of doubt, Product refers to the active pharmaceutical ingredient only and does not include any packaging or labeling. {***} Confidential portions of this exhibit have been redacted and filed separately with the Commission pursuant to a confidential treatment request in accordance with Rule 24b-2 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. Page 1 of 28 "Product Data" means all data possessed by HEMISPHERX relating to the use of Interferon alfa-n3 (human leukocyte derived) to treat patients in the Field and which is needed to obtain regulatory approval in the Territory.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Product" is modified to explicitly exclude packaging and labeling. This contradicts section IV.G where Hemispherx warrants that the Product meet all labelling requirements for the country in which the product is intended to be sold. The term "Product" when used in IV.G. now creates an in-text contradiction. If the "Product" is just the active pharmaceutical ingredient, then that means SCIEN is responsible for making sure the labeling is correct, but IV.G states that HEMISPHERX is. This creates uncertainty as to which party is responsible for correct labeling.
perturbation.contradicted_text: G. HEMISPHERX warrants that the Product (i) shall conform to the specifications set out in the SCIEN purchase order for Product and (ii) shall meet all, if any, reasonably applicable regulatory requirements in the Territory once Product is approved. In the Direct Access/ EAP setting, the Product that HEMISPHERX supplies must confirm with all manufacturing and regulatory requirements (including labelling) for the country in which said Product is intended to be sold. SCIEN's acceptance of the Product shall relieve HEMISPHERX from the obligations arising from this warranty
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of "Product" to exclude packaging and labeling directly contradicts section G, which states that HEMISPHERX warrants that the Product meets all labeling requirements. The change introduces ambiguity and contradiction by implying SCIEN is responsible for labeling while G implies HEMISPHERX is. This creates a strong in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology, as the term 'Product' is now used with conflicting implications regarding labeling responsibilities. If 'Product' only refers to the active pharmaceutical ingredient, it directly impacts the clarity and enforceability of the warranty in section G.

perturbation.original_text: A. Subject to the condition above, HEMISPHERX hereby grants SCIEN the exclusive license to sell, market, and distribute Product for use in the Field in the Territory for Direct Access/EAP and Regulatory Agency-Approved (RAA) purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: A. Subject to the condition above, HEMISPHERX hereby grants SCIEN the exclusive license to promote, market, and supply Product for use in the Field in the Territory for Direct Access/EAP and Regulatory Agency-Approved (RAA) purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The term "sell, market, and distribute" is changed to "promote, market, and supply." Although similar, "promote" and "supply" could be interpreted differently than "sell" and "distribute". The term distribution is replaced with supply in this section. However, section III.B.7 uses the term "Sales, Marketing, and Distribution Plan" in which it says that SCIEN must prepare and provide this plan. Distribution can be interpreted differently from supply, since supply means simply supplying the product, while distribution involves a larger scope that includes things such as distribution channels. SCIEN could argue that they are only responsible for supplying, not distribution, based on the modification to the contract, but Hemispherx could argue that since the Sales, Marketing, and Distribution plan requires SCIEN to create a "Distribution" plan, the term is in-text contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Prepare and provide a 3-year post regulatory approval Sales, Marketing, and Distribution Plan including a 3-year minimum sale forecast and a committed-dollar field sales force, product manager and marketing budget to be agreed by both Parties and a non-binding 12 month Product forecast no later than six (6) months prior to the anticipated registration and subsequent launch date for each Product, also to be agreed by both parties,
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies SCIEN's responsibility to 'sell, market, and distribute' the product. The changed text modifies this to 'promote, market, and supply.' This introduces a contradiction with the 'Sales, Marketing, and Distribution Plan' requirement outlined in Section 7 of the contradicted text. The original term 'distribute' has a broader scope than 'supply.' Since the change to the contract now stipulates that SCIEN is only responsible for supplying the product, it directly contradicts the expectation of a comprehensive 'Distribution Plan,' creating ambiguity about SCIEN's obligations and potentially leading to disputes.

perturbation.original_text: HEMISPHERX will have the option at any time to buy out this Agreement. If exercised within the first two (2) years HEMISPHERX will pay SCIEN three (3) times the Product sales for the preceding 12 months. If exercised after year 3, HEMISPHERX will pay SCIEN two (2) times the Product sales for the preceding 12 months.
perturbation.changed_text: HEMISPHERX will have the option at any time to buy out this Agreement. If exercised within the first two (2) years HEMISPHERX will pay SCIEN three (3) times the Product profits for the preceding 12 months. If exercised after year 3, HEMISPHERX will pay SCIEN two (2) times the Product profits for the preceding 12 months.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Product sales" is changed to "Product profits." It's unclear whether Product sales and Product profits are referring to the same calculation, so this causes an in-text contradiction because this changes the term of calculating how much Hemispherx would pay Scien. The original text states "Product sales", which would mean the total revenue from the sales of the product. The change text states "Product profits", which would mean revenue from the sales of the product minus any costs associated with it. There is a material difference between sales and profit. In section V.A, SCIEN has to provide quarterly reports that include the number of Product Units sold and the Sales Price. This section does not require SCIEN to provide any data on the cost of the product, creating a contradiction on how to calculate profits, because it is not defined in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. Within 30 days following the end of each calendar quarter after execution of the Agreement, SCIEN will provide HEMISPHERX with quarterly reports on the number of Product Units sold and the Sales Price during the preceding three months, key market place issues and successes, regulatory and reimbursement subjects and revisions to the sales and marketing plans.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Product sales" with "Product profits." Section A requires SCIEN to provide reports including the number of "Product Units sold and the Sales Price," but does not require SCIEN to provide any data on costs, thereby making it impossible to calculate "Product profits" as newly defined. The change introduces ambiguity and a material contradiction because the data necessary to calculate the new payment term is not defined in the agreement, and contradicts the data SCIEN is required to report.
